data.title: MicroStrategy stands to gain from potential Nasdaq 100 inclusion: Bernstein
data.description:
MicroStrategy's (NASDAQ:MSTR) growth engine could shift into overdrive, Bernstein suggested in a recent note, as the company's potential inclusion in the Nasdaq 100 Index might amplify its bitcoin-driven momentum and attract more capital for further bitcoin (BTC-USD) purchases.

Shares of the self-proclaimed bitcoin (BTC-USD) development company co-founded by Michael Saylor have already surged some 550% this year, as it intensified its bold strategy to raise capital exclusively for buying and holding bitcoin, the price of which has also been on a tear.

"With potential inclusion in Nasdaq100 and QQQ, we expect more visibility and recognition beyond fresh ETF inflows," analyst Gautam Chhugani, who has an Outperform rating on MSTR, wrote in a note to clients.

"And if the Nasdaq100 inclusion happens, the market will likelyset its sight on S&P 500 inclusion for 2025," though that may be a challenge "due to profitability of its software business."

Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas noted that MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) will likely be added to the Nasdaq 100 on Dec. 23 with a 0.46% weight in the index, according to a recent post on X.

In all, the MicroStrategy (MSTR) "flywheel is going to further accelerate from here," Chhugani contended, as the U.S. set to ratchet up its focus on crypto regulation after President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.

Bernstein's Outperform rate diverges from the SA Quant system rating of Hold and the average SA analyst of Sell, but aligns with the average Wall Street analyst rating of Strong Buy.

(MSTR) shares edged up 0.5% in premarket trading.

data.title: GoGold Resources GAAP EPS of $0.002, revenue of $10.41M
data.description:
• GoGold Resources press release (OTCQX:GLGDF): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.002.
  • Revenue of $10.41M (+83.0% Y/Y).
  • Highlights for the year ending September 30, 2024: With Parral revenue increasing by 20%, generating $37 million from the sale of 1.4 million silver equivalent ounces.
  • Production of 1,482,391 silver equivalent ounces, consisting of 553,382 silver ounces, 8,700 gold ounces, 468 copper tonnes, 316 zinc tonnes.
  • Adjusted cash cost per silver equivalent ounce of $17.62.
  • Adjusted all in sustaining cost per silver equivalent ounce of $24.15.

data.title: Crown Laboratories commences tender offer for Revance Therapeutics at $3.10 per share
data.description:
• Crown Laboratories commenced a cash tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares of Revance Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RVNC) at a price of $3.10 per share in cash.
  • The Revance (RVNC) Board believed the certainty of value provided by the $3.10 cash offer was in the best interest of stockholders.
  • The tender offer is being made in connection with the amended and restated merger agreement, and will expire on January 13, 2025, unless extended.
  • The stock price increased 2% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Optimism among retail investors falls in latest AAII survey
data.description:
Investors’ optimism about the short-term outlook for the stock market decreased during the week, showed the latest AAII Sentiment survey.

A total of 43.3% of respondents, as per the AAII Sentiment survey, showed optimism, compared to 48.3% in the last week. This week’s figure is above the historical average of 37.5%.

The percentage of those surveyed who felt bearish also increased slightly, with the figure standing at 31.7% for the week ended Dec. 11, versus 30.7% last week.

During the week, investors turned their focus on the latest data including consumer price inflation reading as well as November's U.S. jobs report that pointed to a slowing yet solid labor market. The data came ahead of the Federal Reserve monetary policy committee meeting, the last of the year.

Looking into earnings, Oracle (ORCL) disappointed the market with fiscal second-quarter results that were slightly below expectations. The cloud software company closed nearly 7% lower on Tuesday.

Neutral sentiment stood at 25% for the week, versus 21% last week, who saw no change in market direction.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII) since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

The S&P 500 Index (SP500) rose marginally in the last one week, while Dow Jones Industrial Average Index (DJI) fell over 1%. NASDAQ Composite Index (COMP:IND) rose 1.7% and NASDAQ 100-Index (NDX) gained 1.6%.

data.title: Palantir initiated at Neutral on stout growth, high valuation: Baird
data.description:
Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) was initiated with a Neutral rating by Baird as the financial services firm is impressed with its artificial intelligence-powered offerings and revenue growth but remains wary of its high valuation.

Palantir shares have surged more than 300% year to date as the software company has expanded from government contracts to capturing a larger share of commercial revenue as well.

"Its AIP platform, launched in 2023, has catalyzed U.S. Commercial growth, which surged 54.3% YOY in Q3, accelerating total revenue growth to 30%," said Baird analyst William Power, in an investor note. "PLTR has excelled at actually putting generative AI applications into production, which is where we expect most value to be extracted in the coming years."

Baird set a $70 price target on the stock.

U.S. government agencies continue to provide a strong base for Palantir as it has increased nearly 40% year over year to account for 44% of total revenue. With an incoming administration focused on government efficiency, Baird believes Palantir could be a "force multiplier" in that department.

"PLTR is currently trading at 48x our 2025 revenue forecast, the high end of software," Power said. "PLTR has gained 313% YTD vs. the S&P 500 at 27%, suggesting high expectations, though we also acknowledge the strong, accelerating operating momentum."

Baird forecast Palantir's full-year revenue increasing 26% and another 24% in 2025 to reach $3.5B.

data.title: POET Technologies prices capital raise of about $25M via securities offering
data.description:
• POET Technologies (NASDAQ:POET) announces to complete a non-brokered public offering of 5M units at $5.00 per unit to raise $25 million.
  • Each unit will comprise one common share of the company and one-half of one common share purchase warrant, with each warrant being exercisable to acquire one common share at a price of US$6.00 (C$8.50) for a period of five years from the date of issuance.
  • The issue price represents a premium over the closing price of the common shares on the TSX Venture Exchange as on December 11, 2024.

  • The company intends to use the proceeds of the offering for working capital and general corporate purposes.

  • The company intends to close the offering on or about December 19, 2024.
  • POET shares were trading +1.45% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Scilex dips 8% on $17M direct stock offering
data.description:
• Scilex Holding (NASDAQ:SCLX) entered into a definitive agreement with certain institutional investors for the purchase and sale of an aggregate of 26.36M shares and ~2.4M warrants.

  • The warrants are being sold at a combined offering price of $0.59 per share.

  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about December 13, 2024.

  • The gross proceeds are expected to be approximately ~$17M.

  • Net proceeds together with its existing cash and cash equivalents and short-term investments will be used for working capital and general corporate purposes.

  • The stock price declined ~8% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Archer Aviation unveils new strategic partnership as part of its defense program
data.description:
Archer Aviation (NYSE:ACHR) announced on Thursday that it entered a strategic partnership with Anduril to develop a next-generation aircraft for defense applications.

The first product from the partnership is expected to be a hybrid-propulsion, vertical-take-off-and landing aircraft that will target a potential program of record from the United States Department of Defense.

Archer's (ACHR) ability to rapidly develop advanced VTOL aircraft using existing commercial parts and supply chains and Anduril's deep expertise in artificial intelligence, missionization, and systems integration is anticipated to accelerate speed to market for critical hybrid VTOL capabilities at a lower cost of more traditional alternatives.

The company said its efforts on the project will be part of its new Archer Defense program. To support the initiative and for other general corporate purposes, Archer (ACHR) raised $430 million in additional equity capital today, with participation from Stellantis (STLA), United Airlines (UAL), and new institutional investors, including Wellington Management and Abu Dhabi investment holding company 2PointZero.

Shares of Archer Aviation (ACHR) were down 4.85% in premarket trading.

data.title: Great Elm Capital raises $13.2 million of equity at net asset value
data.description:
• Great Elm Capital (NASDAQ:GECC) on Thursday said it has issued about 1.1 million shares of the company’s common stock at $12.06 per share, for a gross purchase price of $13.2 million, to Summit Grove Partners (SGP).
  • Great Elm Group made a $3.3 million investment in SGP, further aligning the company’s investment manager with shareholders.
  • GEG +1.12% premarket to $1.79.
  • Source: Press Release
